title: Executive Order 12331—President's Foreign Intelligence Advisory Board

flags.justification: Members are appointed from among 'trustworthy and distinguished citizens outside the Government,' purely advisory in function.
Sec. 3 requires reporting 'periodically, but at least semi-annually.'
Single coherent purpose: intelligence community oversight and advice.
A purely advisory body with no compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A standing oversight function for an ongoing government need, evergreen by nature, consistent with EO 10938.
This creates independent oversight of intelligence agencies rather than removing any existing oversight.
Sec. 5 grants the Board access to all information necessary from any agency, combined with independent, non-government membership — a genuine external check.

Sec. 2 authorizes the Board to 'continually review the performance of all agencies' engaged in intelligence — a broad, ongoing, textually-claimed oversight mandate over the entire intelligence community, consistent with EO 10938's equivalent finding.
The Board advises rather than commands intelligence agencies.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 1/16 = 6.25% — identical to EO 10938's score, reflecting the near-identical structural design of this re-established version of PFIAB.
